name: Real Estate, Education and Community Housing, INC.
description_short: HUD-approved nonprofit offering free homebuyer education, foreclosure counseling, and down payment assistance to low-to-moderate income families in Florida.
description_long:
Real Estate, Education And Community Housing, Inc. (R.E.A.C.H.) is a Florida-based 501(c)(3) nonprofit organization founded in January 2006. The organization is HUD-approved, operates as a Foreclosure and Housing Counseling Agency, and maintains an affiliation with HomeFree-USA. Their mission centers on empowering low-to-moderate income individuals and families to achieve homeownership and financial stability through education, counseling, and grant assistance.

R.E.A.C.H. offers a comprehensive suite of programs including HUD-approved homebuyer education workshops using NeighborWorks curriculum, foreclosure and eviction prevention counseling, credit mitigation and financial wellness education, down payment assistance guidance, post-closing home maintenance classes, and money management counseling. They also facilitate workshops with local mortgage lenders, real estate professionals, and attorneys. Their Foreclosure Counseling and Intervention Programs help families avoid foreclosure by providing evaluation of options and resources. The Credit Mitigation Program assists with budgeting, savings, and credit issues, with A Second Chance Grant available for past-due accounts subject to pre-qualification.

What distinguishes R.E.A.C.H. is their multilingual service delivery—classes and counseling are provided in clients' native languages whenever possible—and their integration of local financial professionals into the educational process. Their NeighborWorks curriculum is specifically approved by Palm Beach County Commission on Affordable Housing. They operate at no cost to clients and maintain transparent fee structures. The organization's dual focus on prevention (foreclosure counseling) and advancement (homeownership education) addresses both crisis and opportunity.

R.E.A.C.H. is genuinely positioned as a nonprofit resource for financially vulnerable populations with no profit incentive. However, their service area appears geographically concentrated (references to Palm Beach County, West Palm Beach, Florida), which may limit accessibility for those outside this region. Website navigation is somewhat cluttered, making it difficult for first-time visitors to quickly identify specific program eligibility and intake processes. Their grant programs are subject to pre-qualification and funding availability, meaning assistance is not guaranteed.
pros: HUD-approved foreclosure and housing counseling agency with government recognition
Completely free homebuyer education workshops and counseling with no hidden fees
Multilingual program delivery in clients' native languages when possible
NeighborWorks curriculum for homebuyer education approved by Palm Beach County Commission
A Second Chance Grant program available to pay off past-due accounts for qualified applicants
Integration of local mortgage lenders, real estate professionals, and attorneys into workshops
Dual-focus programs addressing both foreclosure prevention and homeownership advancement
Established nonprofit (501(c)(3)) with no profit incentive since 2006
cons: Service area appears limited to Florida, primarily Palm Beach County region
Website navigation is cluttered and difficult to navigate; unclear program intake process
Grant programs subject to pre-qualification and funding availability—assistance not guaranteed
Limited information available on website about specific down payment assistance award amounts
No clear response time or turnaround expectations documented for counseling services
best_for: First-time homebuyers with low-to-moderate incomes in the Florida area seeking free education
Homeowners facing foreclosure or eviction who need counseling and intervention options
Individuals with credit challenges looking for free credit mitigation and financial wellness support
Non-English speakers needing multilingual homebuyer and financial counseling
diagnosis: R.E.A.C.H. is best for low-to-moderate income individuals and families in Florida seeking free, nonprofit housing counseling and education with no profit motive. The main caveat is geographic limitation to the Florida area and dependency on grant funding availability, which means assistance programs are not universally guaranteed despite qualification.
services: HUD-approved homebuyer education workshops (8-hour virtual and in-person classes)
Credit mitigation and financial wellness education
A Second Chance Grant program for past-due account payoff
Referral services to connect qualified clients with programs
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
address: 8409 N Military Trl Ste 111, West Palm Beach, FL 33410
